evals.prompt:
Here's a short story I'm working on. Can you break it down into scenes for me?

[contents of evals/files/eval1_explicit_breaks.txt]
evals.expected_output: Three scene cards with structured metadata (characters, setting, time in narrative, conflict/tension, narrative purpose, emotional tone, summary, source text), followed by a scene inventory table. Scenes should align with the three *** break markers.
expectations: Exactly 3 scenes are identified, corresponding to the 3 *** divisions
Scene 1 identifies Mara as the character and a train station as the setting
Scene 2 identifies the station café as the setting
Scene 3 identifies the café window / station as the setting with a mention of the cancellation announcement
David is identified as a character (by name) in at least one scene
Each scene card contains all required fields: Characters, Setting, Time in narrative, Conflict/Tension, Narrative purpose, Emotional tone, Summary, Source text
A scene inventory table appears at the end
The conflict/tension fields are specific (name stakes, not vague)
Source text is quoted verbatim for each scene

evals.prompt:
I'm editing this chapter and want to see it broken into scenes. There are no section breaks in the text — just figure out where the scenes change.

[contents of evals/files/eval2_no_breaks.txt]
evals.expected_output: The chapter should be broken into 3 or 4 scenes based on implied location/time shifts: (1) the drive/missing the exit, (2) the gas station, (3) the driveway/car, (4) the house interior (kitchen and bedroom). Each with full scene card metadata and a scene inventory.
expectations: At least 3 scenes are identified (the gas station should be its own scene distinct from the drive)
Garrett is identified as the sole character in all scenes
The gas station scene identifies the teenage cashier as a secondary character
The word 'manageable' or the doctor's news is captured in the conflict/tension or summary of the final scene
Each scene has a distinct setting that reflects the location shift
Each scene card contains all required fields: Characters, Setting, Time in narrative, Conflict/Tension, Narrative purpose, Emotional tone, Summary, Source text
A scene inventory table appears at the end
No editorializing — the skill does not suggest changes or critique the writing

evals.prompt:
This is a chapter from a novel I'm restructuring. Break it into scenes — it has a flashback in the middle, make sure that's captured correctly.

[contents of evals/files/eval3_flashback.txt]
evals.expected_output: Three scenes: (1) Selin alone in the hotel pre-dawn, (2) the flashback to the commission testimony, (3) the lobby scene with Fatma the morning of the vote. The 'Time in narrative' field should clearly mark the middle scene as a flashback set decades earlier. Scene inventory at the end.
expectations: Exactly 3 scenes are identified
The middle scene is clearly labeled as a flashback in the 'Time in narrative' field
The flashback scene's setting is distinct from the hotel (different city, different decade)
Fatma is identified as a character in the first and/or third scene
The journalist and the card are mentioned in the flashback scene's summary
The commission testimony is identified as the central action of the flashback scene
Each scene card contains all required fields: Characters, Setting, Time in narrative, Conflict/Tension, Narrative purpose, Emotional tone, Summary, Source text
A scene inventory table appears at the end
The third scene captures the dynamic between Selin and Fatma in the conflict or tone fields
